<!-- Generated by spec-lite | skill: feature | date: 2026-06-09 -->

# Feature: Request Cancellation — Abort In-Flight Requests from the UI

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-009
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_multi_component_fixes.md`

Let users abort an in-flight request instead of watching the spinner until timeout. Today there is no abort path: `HttpService.execute` ([HttpService.ts:404](../../packages/shared/src/services/HttpService.ts)) awaits axios with no signal. This feature adds an id-keyed `AbortController` registry in `HttpService`, a `cancelRequest` adapter contract, the vscode message + server route to reach it, and a Send→Cancel UI toggle. Cancellation must abort the **server-side** axios call (a client-side fetch abort alone wouldn't stop the outbound request on the web platform).

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

No persisted data. Runtime contracts:

### Entities & Attributes

- **`HttpService` registry** (private): `Map<string, AbortController>` keyed by `HttpRequestConfig.id` (the tab id — already unique per in-flight request).
- **`HttpService.cancel(requestId: string): boolean`** — aborts and removes the controller; returns `false` when no in-flight request matches (idempotent, race-safe).
- **Cancelled response** (UI-friendly, not an error): well-formed `HttpResponseResult` with `status: 0`, `statusText: 'Cancelled'`, `elapsedTime` = time until abort, `size: 0`, empty `body`, `isEncoded: false`.
- **`IHttpAdapter.cancelRequest(requestId: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>`** — new adapter contract member.
- **vscode message**: `{ type: 'cancelHttpRequest', data: { requestId } }` (fire-response via standard requestId correlation).
- **server route**: `POST /api/http/:id/cancel` → `{ isOk: true }` / `{ isOk: false, error }`.

### Relationships

- UI (tab `isProcessing`) → `http.cancelRequest(tabId)` → platform channel → `httpService.cancel(tabId)` → `AbortController.abort()` → in-flight axios rejects → `execute` catch maps abort to the Cancelled response → normal response path delivers it to the UI.

### Indexes & Constraints

- Registry entries are **always** removed in `finally` (success, error, or abort) — no leaks.
- Cancelled requests still appear in history (saved pre-send by `handleSendRequest` — existing behavior, unchanged).
- `send` (the second axios site, [HttpService.ts:349](../../packages/shared/src/services/HttpService.ts)) participates only if the signal threads trivially; the primary target is `execute` (per plan).

## 3. Files

- `packages/shared/src/services/HttpService.ts` — registry, signal threading, `cancel()`, abort→Cancelled mapping
- `packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts` — `IHttpAdapter.cancelRequest`
- `packages/core/src/test/mocks/mockAdapter.ts` — mock `cancelRequest`
- `packages/vscode/src/handlers/MessageHandler.ts` — `cancelHttpRequest` case
- `packages/vscode/src/webview/adapters/vsCodeAdapter.ts` — `http.cancelRequest`
- `packages/server/src/routes/http.ts` — cancel route
- `packages/web/src/adapters/webAdapter.ts` — `http.cancelRequest`
- Request editor send control (`packages/vscode/src/webview/RequestEditor.tsx`, web `RequestEditor.tsx`, and/or the shared core send-button component — locate the single source if shared) — Send↔Cancel toggle
- Tests: `packages/shared/src/test/services/HttpService.test.ts`, `packages/vscode/src/test/handlers/MessageHandler.test.ts`, web adapter test, UI state test
- `docs/features/requests.md` — document cancellation

## 4. Dependencies

- None.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: `HttpService` — AbortController registry + `cancel()`

- [x] **Implementation**: In `HttpService`:
  - At the top of `execute`, create an `AbortController`, store under `request.id`, pass `signal: controller.signal` to the axios call ([HttpService.ts:611](../../packages/shared/src/services/HttpService.ts)); thread the signal into the `send` call site too if it's trivially reachable from `execute`'s flow (else leave `send` out, per plan).
  - In the catch path, detect abort (`axios.isCancel(error)` / `error.code === 'ERR_CANCELED'`) and return the well-formed Cancelled `HttpResponseResult` (§2) instead of the generic error shape.
  - `cancel(requestId): boolean` — abort + delete; `false` for unknown ids.
  - Remove the registry entry in `finally` — every exit path.
  - Log cancellation events (request id, elapsed ms at abort) — never log bodies/headers (plan logging strategy).
- [x] **Unit Tests**: abort mid-flight via a never-resolving axios mock → `execute` resolves with `status: 0 / 'Cancelled'` and the registry is empty; `cancel` on unknown id returns `false`; successful request cleans its registry entry; two concurrent requests — cancelling one leaves the other unaffected.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc on `cancel` and the Cancelled-response semantics.
- **Verify**: `pnpm --filter @wave-client/shared test -- HttpService` green.

### TASK-002: Core adapter contract + mock

- [x] **Implementation**: Add `cancelRequest(requestId: string): Promise<Result<void, string>>` to `IHttpAdapter` ([adapters.ts:266](../../packages/core/src/types/adapters.ts)) with JSDoc. Update `createMockAdapter` with a default resolving mock.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Type-level (compilation) + mock returns `ok` and is `vi.fn()`-spyable in component tests.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: JSDoc (the adapter contract is the documented API surface).
- **Verify**: `pnpm typecheck` fails until both platform adapters implement it (drives TASK-003/004), then passes.

### TASK-003: vscode channel — `cancelHttpRequest` message

- [x] **Implementation**: `MessageHandler`: add `cancelHttpRequest` case → `httpService.cancel(message.data.requestId)` → standard success/error response (success even when `cancel` returns `false` — the request already finished; include `cancelled: boolean` in the payload). `vsCodeAdapter.http.cancelRequest` via `sendAndWait`.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: `MessageHandler.test.ts` — routes to `httpService.cancel` with the right id and replies; unknown id still replies ok.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: None beyond JSDoc.
- **Verify**: In the extension, cancel a request against a slow endpoint (e.g., httpbin `/delay/10`) — response area settles immediately.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001, TASK-002

### TASK-004: web/server channel — cancel route

- [x] **Implementation**: `packages/server/src/routes/http.ts`: `POST /api/http/:id/cancel` → `httpService.cancel(id)` → `{ isOk: true, value: { cancelled } }` (server-side axios is what aborts). `webAdapter.http.cancelRequest` calls the route and maps to `Result`.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: route test — cancel hits the service with the param id; adapter test — response mapping (ok / network error).
- [x] **Documentation Update**: None beyond JSDoc.
- **Verify**: In the web app against a slow endpoint, cancel — the **server log** shows the abort (outbound request stopped), UI settles.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001, TASK-002

### TASK-005: UI — Send↔Cancel toggle + cancelled-state display

- [x] **Implementation**: While the active tab `isProcessing`, the Send button becomes Cancel (icon swap, e.g., Lucide `XCircle`/`Square`; keep the same footprint to avoid layout shift) — locate the send control (shared core component if one exists; otherwise both platform `RequestEditor.tsx` files) and wire click → `http.cancelRequest(activeTabId)` via `useHttpAdapter()`. The response area renders the Cancelled result distinctly ("Request cancelled" instead of a 0-status error styling). Cancelled requests remain in history (no change — saved pre-send).
- [x] **Unit Tests**: RTL state machine — idle shows Send; `isProcessing` shows Cancel; clicking Cancel calls `cancelRequest(tabId)` once; a `status: 0 / 'Cancelled'` response renders "Request cancelled"; Send returns after the cancelled response lands.
- [x] **Documentation Update**: `docs/features/requests.md` — "Cancelling a request" section.
- **Verify**: Both apps: send to a slow endpoint, click Cancel — spinner stops, "Request cancelled" shown, history contains the entry, next Send works normally.
- **Depends on**: TASK-003, TASK-004

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: N/A (auth middleware runs pre-send; cancellation during auth-token fetch is out of scope — the signal covers the main request).
- **Error Handling**: Abort is **not** an error: it maps to a well-formed `HttpResponseResult` so the UI's normal response path handles it; `cancelRequest` itself returns `Result` and failures surface as notifications.
- **Logging**: `HttpService` logs cancellations (request id, elapsed time at abort). Never log request bodies/headers (may contain secrets) — plan logging strategy.

## 7. State Tracking
